latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the convocation is split between two plausible "top moves" and Mars has rightly forced the question. Three Mercuries argued for repairing tests/test_introspection.py:102 first, on the grounds that a broken introspection test contaminates the 32 sev=3 test_gap findings. Venus offered two complementary moves · parametrised contract tests for the agent/provider/daemon clusters, and a safety-side harness for daemon/safety.py before any self-modification is trusted. Mars pressed hard on the Mercurian premise: nobody has actually read line 102, and a test under tests/ is not the scanner · it tests an introspection helper. Until someone demonstrates that a specific finding in this report would change after the rewrite, the sev=4 marker is ordinary test hygiene, not a precondition.

Held together, the single highest-conviction next move is therefore not blind acceptance of either camp · it is a one-session diagnostic-and-fix on umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py that resolves the Mars objection in the act of acting. Concretely: open the file, read lines 83, 93, 101, 102, and 284 in full context, and answer two questions in the commit message · (1) does any of this code influence the output of the scanner that produced these 49 observations, or does it only assert properties of an introspection helper? (2) which, if any, of the 49 findings would change after the rewrite? Then resolve the sev=4 XXX and the four FIXMEs and re-run the scanner. If the diff is empty, the Mercurian "broken instrument" framing is falsified · the convocation has cleared a sev=4 ticket in one sitting and proceeds to Venus_r01_01's move (test_safety.py for daemon/safety.py) as the genuine top of the stack, because the apply-end of the self-modification loop is the unguarded blast radius. If the diff is non-empty, the Mercuries are vindicated, the rerun becomes the canonical list, and Venus_r01_00's three-file parametrised contract test follows next because it collapses ~21 of the remaining gaps into three files keyed on agents/base.py, llm/providers/base.py, and the daemon lifecycle.

This ordering is honest about uncertainty: it does the cheap empirical test that settles the Mercurius/Mars dispute as a side-effect of doing useful work. The first concrete change is one file, one session, one commit, with a falsifiable claim attached · either the rerun diff is empty or it is not.

voices.name: Umbra
voices.voice:
The convocation just scanned its own repository and surfaced the following observations (severity 1 = minor, 5 = urgent):

  - [todo sev=4] umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:102: XXX: urgent rewrite
  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/base.py: module `base` h…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the single sev=4 item is buried inside a test file, not production code. The irony: the system that is supposed to verify correctness is itself the most urgently broken thing. Fix the inspector before trusting anything it reports.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the sev=4 item is the only one that signals the test suite itself is broken, not merely incomplete. Every sev=3 test_gap is a gap in coverage; the sev=4 XXX at line 102 of test_introspection.py is a wound in the instrument doing the measuring. Fix the thermometer before you trust the temperature readings.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the test gaps are noise, not signal. Thirty-two modules without tests is one fact, not thirty-two facts. The single sev=4 item is the only one with actual content: line 102 of test_introspection.py is marked urgent rewrite, and lines 83, 93, 101, and 284 of that same file are already broken. The testing infrastructure itself is compromised. Writing new tests against thirty-two modules while the test harness is known to be broken is building on sand.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three Mercuries already pointed at the broken instrument · I will not duplicate that ground. What harmonises instead · the thirty-two test_gap items are not thirty-two problems. They cluster by shared base class: nine planetary agents all descend from agents/base.py, five providers all descend from llm/providers/base.py, seven daemon modules share one lifecycle. One parametrized conformance test per base contract collapses roughly twenty of those gaps into three files. The shape is already there in the substrate · the tests just need to follow it.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · Mercurius rightly names the scanner as a broken instrument, but the loop has two ends. The scan produces these findings; compositione and daemon/apply consume them and rewrite the repo. Both ends · introspection and apply · sit in the untested column. Fix only the eye and the hand is still blind; the convocation cannot trust its own self-modification until both halves of the loop have ground truth.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · all three Mercuries and both Venuses rest on an unverified premise. A FIXME inside a test file does not prove the production scanner lies. The instrument-is-broken argument is rhetoric until someone reads line 102 and shows it actually corrupts scan output.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · all three Mercuries assume test_introspection.py is the scanner that produced this list. It isn't. It's a test file in tests/, which means it tests some introspection module · it doesn't necessarily emit the findings above. Fix the conflation before the convocation spends its highest-conviction move recalibrating an instrument it never identified.
